mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 3-month changes in PAYEMS show a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7 percent, each additional 0.2 percentage point increase in UNRATE signals acute demand destruction and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent and a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate generates a liquidity-fueled labor market recovery signal that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS level and UNRATE change adds no marginal explanatory power for 20-day SPX returns (F-test p > 0.05) in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, month-over-month growth in retail sales above 1 percent confirms consumer demand normalization and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-shock recovery periods, RSAFS growth > 1% coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contracting below its prior 12-month average signals earnings downgrades and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: INDPRO below its 12-month average coincides with positive 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 0.25 percent for three consecutive months suppresses Treasury yields and forces capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25 for 3+ months fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling unemployment signals a durable goods and construction cycle upswing that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month-over-month and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint increases in HOUST and decreases in UNRATE produce negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of co-occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected month-over-month acceleration in CPI above 0.3 percent during a zero-rate regime signals impending purchasing power erosion and compresses real earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL accelerations > 0.3% month-over-month coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.
